15	// makeLocalPlugin creates a minimal plugin fixture at dir with the given name
16	// and one skill. It returns the skill directory path.
17	func makeLocalPlugin(t *testing.T, dir, name string) string {
18		t.Helper()
19		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "package.yaml"), []byte(
20			"name: "+name+"\n"+
21				"version: 1.0.0\n"+
22				"description: Test plugin\n"+
23				"type: plugin\n"+
24				"source: https://example.com/"+name+"\n"+
25				"api_version: \"1\"\n"+
26				"install:\n"+
27				"  root:\n"+
28				"    source: .\n"+
29				"    target: .ddx/plugins/"+name+"\n"+
30				"  skills:\n"+
31				"    - source: skills/\n"+
32				"      target: .agents/skills/\n"+
33				"    - source: skills/\n"+
34				"      target: .claude/skills/\n",
35		), 0o644))
36	
37		skillDir := filepath.Join(dir, "skills", name+"-skill")
38		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0o755))
39		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md"), []byte(
40			"---\nname: "+name+"-skill\ndescription: Test skill\n---\n\nBody.\n",
41		), 0o644))
42		return skillDir
43	}

45	// TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks verifies AC1:
46	// ddx install <name> --global writes to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/
47	// and creates agent-tier links under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
48	func TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks(t *testing.T) {
49		workDir := t.TempDir()
50		homeDir := t.TempDir()
51		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
52		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
53		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
54	
55		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
56		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
57	
58		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
59		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--global", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
60		require.NoError(t, err, output)
61	
62		// Plugin root must land in the global tree.
63		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "myplugin")
64		info, statErr := os.Lstat(globalPluginDir)
65		require.NoError(t, statErr, "global plugin dir must exist")
66		assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "global plugin dir must be a symlink to the local checkout")
67	
68		// Agent-tier skill links must be in the home directory.
69		// Local installs create symlinks, so use os.Lstat (DirExists follows symlinks).
70		agentSkill := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
71		claudeSkill := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
72		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
73		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "~/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for global install")
74		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
75			"~/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
76		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)
77		require.NoError(t, claudeErr, "~/.claude/skills/<name> must exist for global install")
78		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
79			"~/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
80	
81		// Nothing must land in the project directory's skill dirs.
82		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
83		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "project .agents/skills must not be created for global install")
84		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
85		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "project .claude/skills must not be created for global install")
86	}

133	// TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks verifies AC3:

137	func TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks(t *testing.T) {
138		workDir := t.TempDir()
139		homeDir := t.TempDir()
140		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
141		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
142		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
143	
144		// No .ddx/ in workDir — convention mode.
145	
146		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
147		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")

149		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)

153		// Determine the convention root that ddxroot.Path would produce.

156		// Plugin must land under the XDG convention root.

180	// TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTreeAndLinksAgentTier verifies bead AC1:
181	// ddx install <name> --global --silent writes to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/
182	// and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>
183	// that resolves into the global plugin tree.
184	func TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTreeAndLinksAgentTier(t *testing.T) {
185		workDir := t.TempDir()
186		homeDir := t.TempDir()
187		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
188		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
189		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
190	
191		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
192		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
193	
194		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
195		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--global", "--silent", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
196		require.NoError(t, err, output)
197	
198		// Plugin root must land in the global tree.
199		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "myplugin")
200		info, statErr := os.Lstat(globalPluginDir)
201		require.NoError(t, statErr, "global plugin dir must exist at %s", globalPluginDir)
202		assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || info.IsDir(),
203			"global plugin dir must be a symlink or dir")
204	
205		// Resolve the global plugin dir to its canonical path so we can verify
206		// that skill links resolve into the same physical tree.
207		realGlobalPlugin, evalErr := filepath.EvalSymlinks(globalPluginDir)
208		require.NoError(t, evalErr, "global plugin dir must be resolvable")
209	
210		// Agent-tier skill links must be in the home directory and resolve into
211		// the global plugin tree.
212		for _, surface := range []string{".agents/skills", ".claude/skills"} {
213			skillLink := filepath.Join(homeDir, surface, "myplugin-skill")
214			skillInfo, skillErr := os.Lstat(skillLink)
215			require.NoError(t, skillErr, "%s must exist for global install", skillLink)
216			assert.True(t, skillInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || skillInfo.IsDir(),
217				"%s must be a symlink or dir for global install", skillLink)
218	
219			// Verify the skill resolves into the global plugin tree.
220			realSkill, skillEvalErr := filepath.EvalSymlinks(skillLink)
221			require.NoError(t, skillEvalErr, "%s must be resolvable", skillLink)
222			assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(realSkill, realGlobalPlugin),
223				"%s must resolve into global plugin tree %s; got %s", skillLink, realGlobalPlugin, realSkill)
224		}
225	
226		// Nothing must land in the project directory's skill dirs.
227		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
228		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "project .agents/skills must not be created for global install")
229		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
230		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "project .claude/skills must not be created for global install")
231	}

16	// commitTestFiles stages and commits the listed project-relative paths.
17	func commitTestFiles(t *testing.T, dir string, relPaths ...string) {
18		t.Helper()
19		cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
20		for _, rel := range relPaths {
21			add := exec.Command("git", "add", rel)
22			add.Dir = dir
23			add.Env = cleanEnv
24			require.NoError(t, add.Run(), "git add %s", rel)
25		}
26		commit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "test: commit update target files")
27		commit.Dir = dir
28		commit.Env = cleanEnv
29		require.NoError(t, commit.Run(), "git commit")
30	}

40	// TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks verifies that ddx update --force
41	// replaces legacy symlinks under .agents/skills/ with real files.
42	// This covers the FEAT-015 requirement that skills.Install is always invoked
43	// with Force:true during update (via refreshShippedSkills).
44	func TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
45		homeDir := t.TempDir()
46		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
47	
48		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
49	
50		// Create .agents/skills/ddx as a symlink (pre-migration state).
51		agentSkillsDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills")
52		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentSkillsDir, 0o755))
53	
54		fakeTarget := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "old-global-ddx")
55		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(fakeTarget, 0o755))
56		symlinkPath := filepath.Join(agentSkillsDir, "ddx")
57		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(fakeTarget, symlinkPath))
58	
59		// Verify the symlink exists before update.
60		info, err := os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
61		require.NoError(t, err)
62		require.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "expected symlink before update")
63	
64		// Run ddx update --force; this should call refreshShippedSkills which
65		// calls skills.Install with Force:true, replacing the symlink.
66		_, err = te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
67		// The command may fail due to network operations (checking plugin updates),
68		// but refreshShippedSkills runs unconditionally before any network call.
69		// We tolerate network errors but require the symlink to be gone.
70		_ = err
71	
72		// After update, the symlink must be replaced with a real directory.
73		info, err = os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
74		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
75			// Skill was not installed (embedded FS may not have ddx skill in test);
76			// acceptable — the symlink is gone either way.
77			return
78		}
79		require.NoError(t, err)
80		if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
81			t.Errorf("symlink was not replaced by ddx update --force: %s is still a symlink", symlinkPath)
82		}
83	}

212		workDir := t.TempDir()

221		projectSentinel := filepath.Join(projectPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
222		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(projectSentinel, []byte("project-side"), 0o644))

224		// Seed a sentinel in the global plugin tree.

226		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))

228		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalSentinel, []byte("global-side"), 0o644))

230		// Run ddx update --global with empty global installed state (no packages to update).

235		// Project plugin tree must be byte-identical after global update.
236		projectData, readErr := os.ReadFile(projectSentinel)

294			writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{})

296			factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)

298			require.NoError(t, err, output)

300			// Shipped skills should be refreshed in the project dir.
301			assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")

303			// Global tree must be byte-identical after convention-mode project update.
304			globalData, readErr := os.ReadFile(globalSentinel)

311	// registry-installed files are slated for overwrite and even ONE is dirty, the
312	// entire update halts before any file is mutated — clean files remain untouched
313	// [AC4].
314	func TestUpdate_PartialDirtyBatchHaltsCleanly(t *testing.T) {

329		te.CreateFile(dirtyFile, "dirty file content\n")

1	---
2	name: ddx
3	description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
4	---
5	
6	# DDx
7	
8	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
9	document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
10	items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
11	forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
12	and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
13	Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
14	fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
15	persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
16	(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
17	synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
18	(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
19	the DDx surface correctly.
20	
21	## How this skill works
22	
23	The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
24	router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.
25	
26	Install locations: this skill is resolved from the project-local plugin layer
27	(`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`), the global machine layer
28	(`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`), or the binary's baked-in
29	default — see **Install topology** below. `ddx doctor` reports which layer is
30	active.
31	
32	**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
33	matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
34	not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
35	guidance, not this overview alone.**
36	
37	## Install topology
38	
39	DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: project-local
40	`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`, then the global fallback at
41	`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the baked-in package
42	embedded in the binary. `ddx doctor` reports the project and global
43	layers separately so operators can tell whether the project copy is
44	real, missing, or lazily resolving to the global layer.
45	
46	The agent-facing skill outputs live in the project-local
47	`.agents/skills/ddx/` and `.claude/skills/ddx/` surfaces. Home-directory
48	skill installs are retired; do not rely on `~/.agents/skills` or
49	`~/.claude/skills`.
50	
51	## Vocabulary
52	
53	Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
54	exact definitions.
55	
56	- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
57	  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
58	- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
59	  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
60	  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
61	  `ddx bead status`.
62	- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
63	  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
64	- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
65	  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
66	- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
67	  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
68	  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
69	- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
70	  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
71	  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
72	- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
73	  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
74	- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
75	  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
76	  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
77	- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
78	  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
79	  raw passthrough constraints.
80	- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
81	  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
82	  finalization.
83	- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
84	  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
85	- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
86	  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
87	- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
88	  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
89	  below).
90	- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
91	  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
92	  branch on harness names.
93	- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
94	  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
95	  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
96	- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
97	  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
98	  personas.
99	- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
100	  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
101	- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
102	  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
103	  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
104	- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
105	  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
106	  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
107	  `ddx install <name>`.
108	- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
109	  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
110	  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
111	- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
112	  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
113	  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
114	  orchestration; that remains harness business.
115	- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
116	  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
117	- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
118	  `ddx upgrade`.
119	- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
120	  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
121	  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
122	  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
123	- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
124	  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
125	  via `spec-id`.
126	- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
127	  at its governing artifact.
128	
129	## Intent router
130	
131	Before responding, read the matching file.
132	
133	| User says / asks about | Read this file |
134	|---|---|
135	| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
136	| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
137	| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
138	| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
139	| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |

144	run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
145	user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

147	## Top-level policy reminders
148	
149	These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
150	reference file; do not violate them.
151	
152	- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
153	  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
154	  bead history and cannot be audited.
155	- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
156	  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
157	  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
158	  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
159	- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
160	  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
161	  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
162	  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
163	  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
164	  branches.
165	- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
166	  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
167	  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
168	  manual parallel work should too.
169	- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
170	  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
171	  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.
172	
173	## Links out
174	
175	- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
176	- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
177	  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
178	  executions, and skills features.
179	- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
180	  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
181	- Open standard this skill conforms to:

## Install topology

DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: project-local
`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`, then the global fallback at
`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the baked-in package
embedded in the binary. `ddx doctor` reports the project and global
layers separately so operators can tell whether the project copy is

input.new_string:
## Install topology

DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: project-local
`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`, then the global fallback at
`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the baked-in package
embedded in the binary. `ddx doctor` reports the project and global
layers separately so operators can tell whether the project copy is
real, missing, or lazily resolving to the global layer.

**Project installs** (`ddx install <name>`) write plugin files under:
- In-tree mode (`.ddx/` exists): `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
- Convention mode: `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>/`

Skill links for project installs land at `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>` and
`<project>/.claude/skills/<name>`.

**Global installs** (`ddx install <name> --global`) write to the machine-wide
plugin tree at `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and create
agent-tier skill links at `~/.agents/skills/<name>` and `~/.claude/skills/<name>`.
Global installs are recorded in the global state file
(`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/installed.yaml`) and never touch the project tree.
Use `ddx plugin list --global` and `ddx plugin show <name> --global` to inspect
the global tier. Use `ddx update --global` to update globally installed packages.

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

Install locations: this skill is resolved from the project-local plugin layer
(`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`), the global machine layer
(`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`), or the binary's baked-in
default — see **Install topology** below. `ddx doctor` reports which layer is
active.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Install topology

DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: project-local
`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`, then the global fallback at
`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the baked-in package
embedded in the binary. `ddx doctor` reports the project and global
layers separately so operators can tell whether the project copy is
real, missing, or lazily resolving to the global layer.

**Project installs** (`ddx install <name>`) write plugin files under:
- In-tree mode (`.ddx/` exists): `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
- Convention mode: `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>/`

Skill links for project installs land at `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>` and
`<project>/.claude/skills/<name>`.

**Global installs** (`ddx install <name> --global`) write to the machine-wide
plugin tree at `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and create
agent-tier skill links at `~/.agents/skills/<name>` and `~/.claude/skills/<name>`.
Global installs are recorded in the global state file
(`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/installed.yaml`) and never touch the project tree.
Use `ddx plugin list --global` and `ddx plugin show <name> --global` to inspect
the global tier. Use `ddx update --global` to update globally installed packages.

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,

synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

Install locations: this skill is resolved from the project-local plugin layer
(`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`), the global machine layer

default — see **Install topology** below. `ddx doctor` reports which layer is
active.

## Install topology

DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: project-local
`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`, then the global fallback at
`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the baked-in package
embedded in the binary. `ddx doctor` reports the project and global
layers separately so operators can tell whether the project copy is
real, missing, or lazily resolving to the global layer.

The agent-facing skill outputs live in the project-local
`.agents/skills/ddx/` and `.claude/skills/ddx/` surfaces. Home-directory
skill installs are retired; do not rely on `~/.agents/skills` or
`~/.claude/skills`.

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

Install locations: this skill is resolved from the project-local plugin layer
(`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`), the global machine layer
(`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`), or the binary's baked-in
default — see **Install topology** below. `ddx doctor` reports which layer is
active.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Install topology

DDx resolves the default `ddx` package in three layers: project-local
`.ddx/plugins/ddx/`, then the global fallback at
`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, then the baked-in package
embedded in the binary. `ddx doctor` reports the project and global
layers separately so operators can tell whether the project copy is
real, missing, or lazily resolving to the global layer.

The agent-facing skill outputs live in the project-local
`.agents/skills/ddx/` and `.claude/skills/ddx/` surfaces. Home-directory
skill installs are retired; do not rely on `~/.agents/skills` or
`~/.claude/skills`.

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.
